title: Tim McGraw (Commentary)
lines: Hey I'm Taylor Swift, and you're listening to
Big Machine Radio
And I'm Becca
And we are celebrating the anniversaries
Of the release of Taylor Swift's albums
Because you know she always tends
To release her albums in October or November
That means there are alot of milestones
To celebrate around this time of year
In fact, it was twelve years ago
That we were first introduced to Taylor
Through her self-titled debut album
You know when you think about it
It's hard to believe it's already been twelve years
And in some ways, it's hard to believe
It's ONLY been twelve years
But it happened October 24, 2006
That's when the world was introduced
To Taylor Swift and all of her incredible songs
Like "Tim McGraw", "Teardrops on My Guitar"
"Picture to Burn", and all of those songs
That laid the foundation for what has become
An incredible career for Taylor Swift
And during this special, we're gonna re-visit
Every single one of those songs
Off of Taylor Swift's debut album
We're also gonna be hearing from Taylor
We have dug back in our archives
And found Taylor talking about this album
And where she was at in her life back in that time
Not surprising, she says "Tim McGraw"
Is a song that she wrote based on one of her
True personal life stories, and in fact
She did't actually think that it would be
As significant in her career as it was
I wrote it about this guy that I was dating
Who was about to go off to college
And I knew we were gonna break up
So I started thinkin' about all the things
I knew that would remind him of me
And, um, you know one of the first things
That came to mind was Tim McGraw
And this song really
I didn't think anyting of it when I wrote it
Certainly didn't think it would be a single
Certainly didn't think it would be a hit single
And, you know, when I played it for my record label
They were just like, "Well, that's your first single."
For alot of people
This was their introduction to Taylor Swift
When this song came on the radio
It's "Tim McGraw" on Big Machine Radio

title: Picture To Burn (Commentary)
lines: This is Big Machine Radio
And we're celebrating the 12 year Anniversary
Of Taylor Swift's debut album
Also called, Taylor Swift
Which she released back in October of 2006
We're playing all the songs off the album
Going back and hearing from Taylor at that time
And you know Taylor will never forget
A fateful meeting with Scott Borchetta
After she had just finished playing at The Bluebird Cafe
Here in Nashville
He came up to me after the show
And was like, uhm, I am working on something
And I want you to be a part of it
But I can't talk to you about it right now
And what that meant was
That he was still at Universal Records
He was a VP at Universal Records
And what that meant was, that he was
Starting up a brand new label
He wanted me to be on it
So that label turned out to be Big Machine Records
We here at Big Machine
Could not be prouder of Taylor
And everything she has gone on to accoplish
Since she released her debut album
Back in 2006
And I'm sure you've heard this song from Taylor
It's "Picture To Burn" on Big Machine Radio

title: Teardrops On My Guitar (Radio Single Remix / Commentary)
lines: Celebration, a week of milestones
We are revisiting all of Taylor Swift's albums and celebrating their anniversaries
It's the 12th year anniversary of Taylor Swift's debut album
And we're playing all of the tracks off of her debut album which came out October 24th, 2006
I know a lot of people could relate to the song
Taylor said, "You know what?
The guy at the time probably didn't know how I felt, but he did after this song came out"
This song that I wrote about this guy, who, I had a big crush on
Sat next to me in class, and um
He would come in and talk about his girlfriend every single day
And how pretty she was, and how happy they were, and
I never told him that I liked him
But I did write a song with his name in it
So, um, I guess his secret's out now
You know, that song's really about hiding how you really feel
Yeah, I'd say the cat was definitely out of the bag after that one
And you know the thing about Taylor's song "Teardrops on My Guitar"?
It was so relatable to so many people as are so many of Taylor Swift's songs
And here it is on Big Machine Radio

title: A Place In This World (Commentary)
lines: Thanks for being here with Big Machine Radio
And celebrating the 12th year anniversary
Of Taylor Swift's debut album being released
We're going back and reliving some of the memories
With Taylor about the album
In fact, Taylor talked about the fact that this debut album
Was written from her perspective as a young woman
And writing about things that meant something to her
Well, you know, there's different songs about different things
And I'll be the first one to stand up and say
Look, I'm 17, never gotten married or had a kid
I'm not gonna write songs about that
But I will write about what I have been through
Or what my friends have been through
You know, at a young age I think I developed
A really keen sense of observation
And just being able to watch people
And how they communicate with each other
And, you know, when I went back and read some of the commentary
That fans had posted about Taylor Swift's debut album
It was obvious that this next song they were able to relate to
On a very personal level
This is called, "A Place In This World" off Taylor Swift's debut album
On Big Machine Radio

title: I’m Only Me When I’m With You (Commentary)

As we reflect back, it's been 12 years
Since she released her self-titled debue album

title: Teardrops On My Guitar (Pop Version / Commentary)
lines: We are celebrating 12 years since Taylor Swift released her self-
Titled debut album, and we've played all the tracks off of the deluxe
Version of the album except for one final song, and it's actually
A remix, a pop version of "Teardrops On My Guitar," and you almost
Have to wonder if maybe Taylor had in the back of her mind where
Her career would be leading, because she says, "You know, when I
Go into the studio, I know exactly what I want out of each song."
There was never a recording session where they were tracking
Instruments that I wasn't there, and, you know, asking, "Hey Nathan
Can you turn this up? I need to hear a little more mandolin," or
You know, different songs that had piano. You know, when I
Write a song, I hear how it's supposed to sound. I can hear
The production, I can hear what the drums are doing, and, you
Know, I can hear what the mandolin's doing, what the bass is
Doing, what everything is doing when I'm writing that song
So, um, usually when we go into the studio, all I have to do is
Sit down with Nathan for about 10 minutes and say, "Look, this
Is how I want this to sound," and he just puts it all to life
Whether it was foreshadowing or just coincidence, I know this song
"Teardrops On My Guitar," it was one that helped introduce a lot
Of people to Taylor Swift's music, because it was the second song
That she ever released off her debut album. Let's go ahead and
Play that final track off of Taylor Swift's debut album. It's "
Teardrops On My Guitar," the pop version, on Big Machine Radio.

title: …Ready For It? (Commentary)
lines: This is Big Machine Radio
I am Becca
And can you believe it has been a year
Since Taylor Swift released her "Reputation" album
We wanted to celebrate by looking at things through your eyes
We asked you, the fans, to let us know
"What is your favorite song off "Reputation?"
Or, "What was your favorite memory from her tour?"
And we're gonna take a look back at this album
Play every song and hear from you
And what you thought
"Hi, my name's Emma
My favorite part of the "Reputation" stadium tour
Was when we were all waiting for Taylor to come out
And that, when she did, the whole crowd just went wild
It was so amazing!"
Yeah, the tension that built up as we were all waiting for Taylor
Was pretty incredible
"Hi, my name's Sean, and I'm from Boston
And I saw Taylor Swift at Gillette Stadium
Night number two, and the best part of the tour
For me, is that very first part of it
When "Ready for It" starts playing
And you realize that Taylor's coming out onto the stage
And, you know, all that waiting that you've been doing for the last couple years
Was all worth it
I still get chills when I think about it
And I can't wait to see her again next time"
Sean, I couldn't agree more
I remember how I felt when she finally hit the stage
It was amazing!
And of course, she kicked off the "Reputation" tour with this song
It's "Ready for It", track one on the album
On Big Machine Radio

title: So It Goes… (Commentary)
lines: Thanks so much for listening the Big Machine Radio
As we celebrate the one year anniversary of
Taylor Swift's Reputation album
And when you look back at this album
It made so many of the year-end list
For example, "People" named it one of the 10 best albums of 2017
So did the "New York Times", also " Time Magazine"
Chose Reputation is one of the top 10 albums of 2017
Not to mention all the awards that it won
It picked up the independent impact award
The favorite pop rock album award at the American Music Awards
It was also named the top selling album at the Billboard Music Awards
So to say that Taylor's Reputation album has been a huge success
With certainly be an understatement
And we wanted to take a look back at the album through your eyes
Find out what were your favorite moments on tour
What were your favorite songs of the album
Fan's call
You know, Sydney
A lot of people agree with you
They love that surprise song
It was the 11th song that she sings every night
During her Reputation tour
And it was always a different song
It might have been "Hey Stephen" or "Tied together with a smile"
"The story of us", "Forever and always"
And I know here in Nashville she sings "Better man"
Which she actually wrote for little big town
And that was always a fun part of the tour
And this next song that we gonna play is actually one
That she didn't include in her setlist for the Reputation tour
But I'll tell you what
When I started looking at all the lyrics on Taylor's Reputation album
This is one of those songs that stood out to me
Kind of marking the fact that Taylor Swift was a full-fledged woman
And her lyrics had grown up and her experiences had grown up
It's called "So it goes" on Big Machine Radio

title: King Of My Heart (Commentary)
lines: Any Taylor Swift fan knows
She always releases her new albums in the fall
That's why this time of year we always have plenty to celebrate
When it comes to album anniversaries
And right now we're celebrating a special one
It's the one year anniversary of Taylor Swift's 'Reputation' album
And so we have enlisted, you, the fans, to let us know
What was your favourite song on the album?
Or your favourite moment from the 'Reputation' tour?
"My name is Giovanni Salazar, I'm from Dallas, Texas
Currently in Philadelphia, and my favourite song from 'Reputation'
Is 'King of My Heart'"
"Hi my name is Joel, or Jo-Jo, I'm from New York
And 'King of My Heart' is my favourite song from 'Reputation'"
And I think like all of Taylor Swift's songs, we all speculate

title: I Forgot That You Existed (Piano/Vocal)
lines: So the clip that you're about to hear is my preparation for a writing session
I was writing with two writers I hadn't ever worked with before
so I wanted to come in with an idea that was pretty much all there, melody and lyrics
And so I had this idea called "I Forgot That You Existed"
and I wanted it to be really simple
I thought it might be a really fun way to open the album, like
basically kind of shrugging off a lot of things that you've been through that have been causing a lot of struggle and pain and just
one day, you wake up and you realize you're indifferent to whatever caused you that pain
And so this is a clip of me just trying to tinkering around on the piano
trying to figure out exactly what I wanted to do with it
and you can hear the end result on the album
but this is where it started
